id.description: Used to identify the template when running the command (projgen create <template-id>).

name.description: Display name, should be a human-friendly version of the id.

description.description: A short description of the template.

version.description: The version of the template, should follow semantic versioning (e.g., "1.0.0")

engineVersion.description: The version of the template engine that this template is compatible with

variables.description: Defines the variables that can be used in the template.

name.description: The name of the variable by which it can be referenced later in the template

message.description: The message to display when prompting the user for input for this variable

type.description: The type of the variable, used to determine how to prompt the user for input

default.description: An optional default value for the variable

required.description: Whether the user must provide a value when prompted

name.description: The name of the variable by which it can be referenced later in the template

message.description: The message to display when prompting the user for input for this variable

type.description: The type of the variable, used to determine how to prompt the user for input

default.description: An optional default value for the variable

required.description: Whether the user must provide a value when prompted

name.description: The name of the variable by which it can be referenced later in the template

message.description: The message to display when prompting the user for input for this variable

type.description: The type of the variable, used to determine how to prompt the user for input

default.description: An optional default value for the variable

name.description: The name of the variable by which it can be referenced later in the template

message.description: The message to display when prompting the user for input for this variable

type.description: The type of the variable, used to determine how to prompt the user for input

required.description: Whether the user must provide a value when prompted, is ignored for single selects (one must be selected there)

options.description: An array of options for the select variable type, should only be provided if the variable type is "select", will be ignored otherwise

multiple.description: Whether the user can select multiple options

steps.description: Defines the steps to execute to scaffold the project with the template.

type.description: Defines what kind of step it is

when.description: An optional condition that determines whether this step should be executed, if not provided the step will always be executed

cwd.description: The directory to run the command in relative to the project root, if not provided it will run in the root of the project

type.description: Defines what kind of step it is

when.description: An optional condition that determines whether this step should be executed, if not provided the step will always be executed

path.description: The path to the file to write, relative to the project root

content.description: The content to write to the file

url.description: An optional url to fetch the content from, if provided it will ignore the content field and use the fetched content for the file instead, can be used to copy a file from github for example

type.description: Defines what kind of step it is

when.description: An optional condition that determines whether this step should be executed, if not provided the step will always be executed

path.description: The path to the file to patch, relative to the project root

operation.description: The operation to perform on the file. Replace can be used to delete the part as well

find.description: The text to find in the file to determine where to apply the patch. The operation will be applied to all instances of the found text in the file. Not used for append and prepend, since they are relative to the whole file, not to a specific part of it

content.description: The content to use for the patch, use empty string and replace to remove the found text

url.description: An optional url to fetch the content from, if provided it will ignore the content field and use the fetched content for the patch instead, can be used to copy a file from github for example

type.description: Defines what kind of step it is

when.description: An optional condition that determines whether this step should be executed, if not provided the step will always be executed

path.description: The path to the JSON file to patch, relative to the project root

operation.description: The operation to perform on the JSON file. set: set the value at the defined path, removing anything that was there before, will create the path if it doesn't exist. append: only works if the value at the defined path is an array or object, will append the provided value to the array, will create the array if it doesn't exist. remove: will remove the value at the defined path

jsonPath.description: The path to the value relative to the root of the json file as an array (e.g., compilerOptions.paths -> ["compilerOptions", "paths"])

value.description: The value to use for the patch, required for "set" and "append" operations, will be ignored for "remove" operation

variable.description: The name of the variable to check the condition against, should reference a variable defined in the template's variables array

operator.description: The operator to use for the condition (e.g., "eq" for equals, "neq" for not equals, "gt" for greater than, etc.). "matches" and "notMatches" check for regex patterns

value.description: The value to compare the variable against, should be of the same type as the variable being checked

alias.description: The alias used to refer to the template from the CLI.

path.description: The path to the template file.

linkedRegistries.description: Paths or URLs to other registries to link to.
